creator: fbsd@www.zefox.net
text:
(In reply to Sulev-Madis Silber from comment #6)

I just noticed this passage:

"ftdi isolates usb ttl adapter is connected with usb end at 13.4 on amd64 and serial end at current on armv7"

Just to confirm, the usb end of the link in your experiment was running
FreeBSD-13.4 and the serial wires were connected to an armv7 box?

If so, and if I further understand your suspicion to be misbehavior
at the USB end while cu/tip isn't supervising the ftdi adapter, the
problem isn't arm at all, but rather a general FreeBSD problem. 

It could be the kernel, the ucom driver or uftdi driver. If I do
understand you correctly, it might help to write the freebsd-kernel list.

Just for the record, I didn't start using usb-serial adapters on FreeBSD
until 2015 when I started a transition to Raspberry Pi 2 servers
from i386 (Via Eden). There was enough other stuff going wrong so that
intermittent flakiness from ft232 and pl2303 adapters didn't register with
me or was attributed to cable noise. I did gradually move away from pl2303
adapters because of a persistent bad reputation and now use only ft232 on
armv7 and arm64 hardware. Still, I've a general feeling that usb on Freebsd
was and may still be somewhat less than perfect 8-)

bob prohaska
